title: Withdraw 4,969,231 ada for Cardano Enterprise Adoption: Ticketing Platform

rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "Phase 1 of the Sellout ticketing platform is already live on Cardano mainnet and includes on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 4,969,231 ada, stated as approximately $1,093,231 at $0.22 per ADA, over 8 months with milestone-gated disbursement." and clears the evidence gates. What would change this vote to YES: Provide independent evidence for: Sellout funded Phase 1 entirely from its own capital with over $130,000 invested; Provide independent evidence for: Sellout has 200,000+ registered users, has issued 250,000+ tickets to date, and currently supports 350+ events annually. Residual uncertainty: Treasury flow signal is in stressed regime (total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals). Confidence context: score -0.12; confidence 75.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Advisory model lean was -0.020 (display cap ±0.05) and had zero influence on the binding score: The proposal is detailed and includes milestone gating, budget line items, audit plans, oversight mechanics, and a repayment structure, but several decision-critical adoption and commercial claims remain proposer-asserted rather than evidenced inside the document. Under the doctrine's hierarchy, treasury stewardship and evidence quality justify a small cautionary nudge despite plausible public-benefit growth.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: Sellout funded Phase 1 entirely from its own capital with over $130,000 invested.
Provide independent evidence for: Sellout has 200,000+ registered users, has issued 250,000+ tickets to date, and currently supports 350+ events annually.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Fund Phase 2 of a production Cardano-native ticketing platform operated by Sellout.io and built by Anvil Development Agency, including secondary marketplace, royalty enforcement, anti-scalping controls, wallet onboarding, organizer tools, audit, and launch campaign.
Recipient: Anvil Development Agency, Inc. and Intersect as budget administrator

Deliverables: Secondary marketplace for listing, pricing, and reselling tickets with on-chain settlement, Smart-contract-level royalty enforcement for artists and venues, Configurable per-event anti-scalping controls including price ceilings, transfer limits, identity-binding, and time restrictions, Custodial wallet onboarding inside Sellout's existing flow with self-custody export planned, Organizer dashboard/tools for resale parameters, ticket lifecycle data, royalty distributions, and ticket verification, Independent third-party smart-contract security audit with report and remediation published, Public launch campaign, trade-show activations, user/artist/venue education materials, and governance reporting, Public on-chain metrics dashboard and quarterly reporting

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Phase 1 of the Sellout ticketing platform is already live on Cardano mainnet and includes on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Sellout funded Phase 1 entirely from its own capital with over $130,000 invested.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 4,969,231 ada, stated as approximately $1,093,231 at $0.22 per ADA, over 8 months with milestone-gated disbursement.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Phase 2 is expected to deliver a secondary marketplace, on-chain royalty enforcement, anti-scalping controls, wallet onboarding for Sellout users, organizer tools, an independent audit, and launch amplification.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Sellout has 200,000+ registered users, has issued 250,000+ tickets to date, and currently supports 350+ events annually.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal projects 12-month post-launch targets of 200+ events on-chain, 75,000+ CIP-68 NFT tickets, 150,000+ on-chain interactions, $6,000,000+ ticket revenue on-chain, and 20,000+ new wallets.
missing: Independent evidence for: Phase 1 of the Sellout ticketing platform is already live on Cardano mainnet and includes on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification.
Independent evidence for: Sellout funded Phase 1 entirely from its own capital with over $130,000 invested.
Independent evidence for: Sellout has 200,000+ registered users, has issued 250,000+ tickets to date, and currently supports 350+ events annually.

Independent evidence for: The proposal projects 12-month post-launch targets of 200+ events on-chain, 75,000+ CIP-68 NFT tickets, 150,000+ on-chain interactions, $6,000,000+ ticket revenue on-chain, and 20,000+ new wallets.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 4,969,231 ada, stated as approximately $1,093,231 at $0.22 per ADA, over 8 months with milestone-gated disbursement." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Phase 1 of the Sellout ticketing platform is already live on Cardano mainnet and includes on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

missing: verified ecosystem benefit or strategic necessity

decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

flags.detail: Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
